x-enumeration-literals.name: Amortized Repayment Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Repayment is calculated so that each installment includes both principal and interest, gradually reducing the loan balance until it reaches zero by the end of the term.

Comment:
Example:
A home loan where the borrower pays a fixed monthly EMI containing both principal and interest.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Interest-Only Repayment Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The borrower pays only the interest during the loan term, and the full principal amount is repaid at maturity.

Comment:
Example:
A commercial loan where the borrower pays monthly interest and repays the entire principal after 3 years.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Equal Principal Repayment Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Each repayment includes a fixed amount of principal, while the interest decreases over time as the outstanding balance reduces.

Comment:
Example:
A loan where the borrower repays €1,000 principal every month, and interest is calculated on the remaining balance.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Bullet Repayment Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The principal and interest are calculated and paid in a single lump-sum payment at the end of the loan term.

Comment:
Example:
A short-term bridge loan repaid fully after 12 months.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Balloon Payment Repayment Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Repayments are calculated so that smaller installments are paid during the loan period, with a large final payment (balloon payment) covering most of the principal.

Comment:
Example:
A business loan where monthly payments cover interest and a portion of principal, with a large final repayment.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Step-Up Repayment Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Repayment amounts are calculated to increase gradually over time, usually aligned with expected growth in the borrowers income.

Comment:
Example:
A young professionals home loan where payments increase every year.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Step-Down Repayment Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Repayment amounts decrease over time, usually because higher payments are made in the earlier period.

Comment:
Example:
A loan where the borrower pays larger installments in the first few years.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Flexible Repayment Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Flexible / Variable Repayment Calculation: Repayment amounts vary depending on interest rate changes, outstanding balance, or borrower payments.

Comment:
Example:
A variable-rate loan where installment amounts change when the interest rate changes.
